text.headline: Eric C. Greene, Ph.D. - Research Chronicle
text.text: A timeline of academic training, research innovations, and scientific contributions.

text.headline: Ph.D. in Biochemistry, Texas A&M University
text.text: Doctoral research under Dorothy Shippen focusing on telomere biology, establishing foundations in molecular biology and biochemistry.

text.headline: Postdoctoral Research, National Institutes of Health
text.text: Postdoctoral training with Kiyoshi Mizuuchi at NIH, Bethesda, Maryland, advancing toward DNA recombination research.

text.headline: Faculty Appointment, Columbia University
text.text: Joined Columbia University Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics as Professor, establishing the Greene Lab.

text.headline: DNA Curtains Technique Development
text.text: Pioneered the DNA curtains technique for high-throughput single-molecule imaging of DNA-protein interactions.

text.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 DNA Interrogation Publication
text.text: Published highly cited research on CRISPR-Cas9 mechanisms (1100+ citations), revealing how Cas9 searches for target DNA sequences.

text.headline: RAD51 and Homologous Recombination Research
text.text: Ongoing research program studying RAD51 recombinase family proteins and DNA repair mechanisms critical for genome integrity.

text.headline: Herbert Irving Comprehensive Cancer Center Member
text.text: Research connections to cancer biology through genome stability and DNA repair mechanisms.
